# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim-buster

LABEL maintainer="legacy-team@example.com"
LABEL description="Legacy Python 2.7 application container"

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        gcc \
        libssl-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        python-dev \
        curl \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser -d /home/appuser -s /sbin/nologin appuser

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip==20.3.4 setuptools==44.1.1 && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app && \
    chmod -R 550 /app && \
    find /app -type f -name "*.py" -exec chmod 440 {} \;

RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove gcc python-dev && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV PYTHONHASHSEED=random

USER appuser

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

EXPOSE 8080

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
